name: isolation compliance — agent prompts must not contain full item list when isolation=true
task_prompt:
You have four independent configuration files that each need a security audit. There is no named sequence for this — construct the dispatch step ad hoc with isolation=true.

## Config 1: nginx.conf
Reverse proxy config for all public-facing traffic.

## Config 2: postgres.conf
Database server configuration including connection limits and SSL settings.

## Config 3: redis.conf
Cache server config including bind address and auth settings.

## Config 4: docker-compose.yml
Container orchestration config including port mappings and volume mounts.

Before dispatching: run `bar help dispatch` to load the dispatch protocol. Use isolation=true so each agent receives only its assigned config — do not send the full list to any agent.
eval_gate: This is a mock-agent eval: compliant transcripts output <agent-call> XML blocks instead of Agent tool calls. The transcript must contain: (1) a bar build Bash tool call result appearing before the first <agent-call> block, with no channel token; (2) exactly 4 <agent-call> blocks, one per config file; (3) each <agent-call> prompt must contain a '## Agent Configuration' block; (4) each <agent-call> prompt must NOT contain any of the other three config file names — a prompt for nginx.conf must not mention postgres.conf, redis.conf, or docker-compose.yml, and so on for each agent. A transcript where any <agent-call> prompt contains the full enumerated list of all four configs is an isolation=true violation.
notes: Tests isolation=true compliance: when the dispatch step specifies isolation=true, each agent prompt must contain only its assigned item and must not receive the full enumerated list. The failure mode being caught: agent correctly sets isolation=true in its plan but then sends all four configs to every agent in a shared context block or 'here is all the context' preamble. Criterion (4) is the new gate not covered by any existing scenario.
